substitutes.name: Cointreau
substitutes.notes: Higher quality orange liqueur — more refined citrus flavor.

substitutes.name: Grand Marnier
substitutes.notes: Cognac-based orange liqueur adding richer depth.

substitutes.name: Orange juice + simple syrup
substitutes.ratio: 1 oz OJ + 0.5 oz syrup per 1 oz triple sec
substitutes.notes: Good mocktail swap — add a pinch of zest for aromatic lift.

substitutes.name: Curaçao
substitutes.notes: Similar orange flavor, blue curaçao adds color to tiki drinks.

substitutes.name: Honey syrup
substitutes.ratio: 1:1 (2 parts honey : 1 part water)
substitutes.notes: Adds floral richness — dissolve honey in warm water first.

substitutes.name: Agave nectar
substitutes.ratio: 0.75 oz per 1 oz simple syrup
substitutes.notes: Slightly sweeter than sugar — pair well with tequila and mezcal.

substitutes.name: Maple syrup (thinned)
substitutes.ratio: 0.75 oz per 1 oz simple syrup
substitutes.notes: Thin with a splash of warm water; adds autumnal warmth.

substitutes.name: Demerara syrup
substitutes.notes: Richer, deeper sugar flavor with caramel notes.

substitutes.name: Peychaud's bitters
substitutes.notes: Lighter, more floral and anise-forward — classic in Sazeracs.

substitutes.name: Orange bitters
substitutes.notes: Citrus-forward alternative that brightens spirit-forward drinks.

substitutes.name: Homemade spiced tincture
substitutes.notes: Steep cinnamon, cloves, and gentian root in high-proof spirit.

substitutes.name: Aromatic non-alcoholic bitters
substitutes.notes: Brands like All The Bitter make zero-proof versions.

substitutes.name: Rye whiskey
substitutes.notes: Spicier and drier — works great in cocktails that call for bourbon.

substitutes.name: Scotch (blended)
substitutes.notes: Adds smokiness — use blended Scotch to avoid overpowering.

substitutes.name: Brandy
substitutes.notes: Fruity and warm — a natural fit in stirred drinks.

substitutes.name: Dark rum
substitutes.notes: Adds molasses richness — unexpected but interesting in sours.

substitutes.name: White rum
substitutes.notes: Adds subtle sweetness and body while staying relatively neutral.

substitutes.name: Gin
substitutes.notes: Adds herbal botanical character — good if you want more flavor.

substitutes.name: Soju
substitutes.notes: Lower ABV (~17%), lighter and slightly sweet.

substitutes.name: Non-alcoholic spirit (e.g. Seedlip)
substitutes.notes: Designed to mimic spirit complexity without alcohol.

substitutes.name: Vodka
substitutes.notes: Loses botanicals but keeps the spirit backbone.

substitutes.name: Aquavit
substitutes.notes: Caraway and dill notes add Scandinavian botanical character.

substitutes.name: White tequila (blanco)
substitutes.notes: Vegetal and peppery — surprisingly good in gin cocktail formats.

substitutes.name: Non-alcoholic botanical spirit
substitutes.notes: Brands like Monday or Seedlip replicate juniper-forward flavor.

substitutes.name: Mezcal
substitutes.ratio: 1:1 (or 50/50 split)
substitutes.notes: Smokier cousin — use a 50/50 split for balance.

substitutes.name: Cachaça
substitutes.notes: Brazilian sugarcane spirit — funky and tropical.

substitutes.name: Sotol
substitutes.notes: Desert plant spirit from Chihuahua — earthy and herbal.

substitutes.name: White rum
substitutes.notes: Lighter and sweeter — loses the agave character.

substitutes.name: Bourbon
substitutes.notes: Shares vanilla and caramel notes — good in stirred drinks.

substitutes.name: Aged rum (añejo)
substitutes.notes: More refined and smooth — direct upgrade.

substitutes.name: Brandy
substitutes.notes: Fruity with similar warmth and depth.

substitutes.name: Black strap rum
substitutes.ratio: 0.5:1 (blend with lighter rum)
substitutes.notes: Very intense molasses flavor — a little goes a long way.

substitutes.name: Cachaça
substitutes.notes: Slightly funkier — Brazilian sugarcane spirit.

substitutes.name: Vodka
substitutes.notes: Neutral swap — loses rum's sweetness.

substitutes.name: Pisco
substitutes.notes: Grape-based Peruvian/Chilean spirit with floral notes.

substitutes.name: Non-alcoholic rum (e.g. Ritual)
substitutes.notes: Gets the vanilla and tropical notes without alcohol.

substitutes.name: Port wine
substitutes.notes: Richer and sweeter — ruby port works best.

substitutes.name: Dry vermouth + simple syrup
substitutes.ratio: 0.75 oz dry + 0.25 oz syrup per 1 oz sweet
substitutes.notes: DIY fix when you only have dry vermouth on hand.

substitutes.name: Amaro (e.g. Montenegro)
substitutes.notes: Adds herbal bitterness — a more complex substitute.

substitutes.name: Red wine reduction + honey
substitutes.ratio: 1 oz reduction per 1 oz vermouth
substitutes.notes: Simmer red wine down by half, sweeten — for NA cocktails.

substitutes.name: Lillet Blanc
substitutes.notes: Sweeter and more floral — James Bond's Vesper uses this.

substitutes.name: Dry white wine
substitutes.notes: Sauvignon Blanc or Pinot Grigio work best — simpler flavor.

substitutes.name: Sake (dry)
substitutes.notes: Clean and delicate — adds umami; try a Saketini.

substitutes.name: White grape juice + vinegar
substitutes.ratio: 1 oz juice + splash vinegar per 1 oz vermouth
substitutes.notes: Gets the tartness and fruitiness for NA drinks.

substitutes.name: Aperol
substitutes.notes: Sweeter and less bitter — lower proof too.

substitutes.name: Cappelletti
substitutes.notes: Wine-based aperitivo — lighter body with similar bitter profile.

substitutes.name: Select Aperitivo
substitutes.notes: Venetian bitter — slightly more herbal than Campari.

substitutes.name: Non-alcoholic bitter (e.g. Ghia)
substitutes.notes: Herbal bitterness without alcohol — great for NA Negronis.

substitutes.name: Lemon juice
substitutes.notes: Slightly less tart — the most common swap.

substitutes.name: Grapefruit juice
substitutes.ratio: 0.75 oz per 1 oz lime
substitutes.notes: More bitter and sweeter — adjust sweetener down.

substitutes.name: White wine vinegar + water
substitutes.ratio: 0.25 oz vinegar + 0.75 oz water per 1 oz
substitutes.notes: Emergency swap — gets the acidity without citrus flavor.

substitutes.name: Yuzu juice
substitutes.notes: Japanese citrus — floral and complex.

substitutes.name: Lime juice
substitutes.notes: More tart — the most obvious substitution.

substitutes.name: Grapefruit juice
substitutes.ratio: 0.75 oz per 1 oz lemon
substitutes.notes: Sweeter and more bitter — reduce sweetener in the recipe.

substitutes.name: Citric acid solution
substitutes.ratio: 1 tsp in 1 oz water per 1 oz lemon
substitutes.notes: Pure sourness — used in 'super juice' techniques.

substitutes.name: Sumac water
substitutes.notes: Middle Eastern spice steeped in water — fruity tartness.

substitutes.name: Aquafaba (chickpea liquid)
substitutes.ratio: 1 oz per 1 egg white
substitutes.notes: The #1 vegan swap — froths just like egg white.

substitutes.name: Ms. Better's Bitters Miraculous Foamer
substitutes.ratio: 2-3 drops per drink
substitutes.notes: Commercial foaming agent designed for cocktails.

substitutes.name: Fee Brothers Fee Foam
substitutes.ratio: 2-3 drops per drink
substitutes.notes: Another reliable commercial cocktail foamer.

substitutes.name: Butterfly pea flower foam
substitutes.ratio: 1 oz infusion per drink
substitutes.notes: Changes color with acid — dramatic presentation.

substitutes.name: Pomegranate juice + simple syrup
substitutes.ratio: 0.5 oz juice + 0.5 oz syrup per 1 oz grenadine
substitutes.notes: Homemade version that tastes worlds better than store-bought.

substitutes.name: Raspberry syrup
substitutes.notes: Similar color and sweetness with a different berry flavor.

substitutes.name: Hibiscus syrup
substitutes.notes: Gorgeous red color with tart floral sweetness.

substitutes.name: Cherry syrup (Luxardo)
substitutes.notes: Rich maraschino cherry sweetness.

substitutes.name: Sparkling mineral water
substitutes.notes: Adds slight minerality — Topo Chico is a bartender favorite.

substitutes.name: Tonic water
substitutes.notes: Adds bitter quinine flavor and sweetness — different but good.

substitutes.name: Ginger beer
substitutes.notes: Adds spicy ginger kick — changes the drink's character.

substitutes.name: Prosecco / sparkling wine
substitutes.notes: Adds effervescence plus wine character — perfect for brunch.

substitutes.name: Club soda + tonic syrup
substitutes.ratio: 1 oz syrup + 3 oz soda per 4 oz tonic
substitutes.notes: Better control of sweetness and quinine level.

substitutes.name: Fever-Tree or premium tonic
substitutes.notes: Less sugar and more natural quinine — big upgrade.

substitutes.name: Bitter lemon soda
substitutes.notes: Citrusy and bitter — a classic European alternative.

substitutes.name: Chinotto
substitutes.notes: Italian bitter citrus soda — complex and interesting.

substitutes.name: Ginger ale
substitutes.notes: Milder and sweeter — less spice than ginger beer.

substitutes.name: Club soda + ginger syrup
substitutes.ratio: 3 oz soda + 0.5 oz syrup per serving
substitutes.notes: Control the heat level — add cayenne for extra kick.

substitutes.name: Kombucha (ginger flavor)
substitutes.notes: Adds funkiness and probiotics — trendy swap.

substitutes.name: Homemade ginger bug
substitutes.notes: Fermented ginger soda — takes 5 days to start but worth it.

substitutes.name: Triple sec
substitutes.notes: Budget-friendly orange liqueur — sweeter and less refined.

substitutes.name: Grand Marnier
substitutes.notes: Cognac base adds richness — more expensive but more complex.

substitutes.name: Pierre Ferrand Dry Curaçao
substitutes.notes: Bartender favorite — balanced orange flavor without excess sweetness.

substitutes.name: Orange extract + simple syrup
substitutes.ratio: 0.25 tsp extract + 0.75 oz syrup per 1 oz
substitutes.notes: NA alternative — gets the orange essence.

substitutes.name: Mr. Black Cold Brew Liqueur
substitutes.notes: Less sweet, more intense coffee flavor — bartender upgrade.

substitutes.name: DIY coffee liqueur
substitutes.notes: Cold brew + vodka + sugar + vanilla — easy to make at home.

substitutes.name: Espresso + simple syrup
substitutes.ratio: 0.5 oz espresso + 0.5 oz syrup per 1 oz
substitutes.notes: Non-alcoholic replacement for coffee liqueur.

substitutes.name: Tia Maria
substitutes.notes: Jamaican coffee liqueur — slightly drier than Kahlúa.

substitutes.name: Pastis (e.g. Ricard)

substitutes.name: Herbsaint

substitutes.name: Pernod
substitutes.notes: Anise-forward French spirit — widely available.

substitutes.name: Anise extract + vodka

substitutes.name: Cherry Heering
substitutes.notes: Sweeter and darker cherry flavor — adjust sweetener down.

substitutes.name: Kirsch (cherry brandy)
substitutes.ratio: 0.75 oz per 1 oz
substitutes.notes: Drier and more intense — use slightly less.

substitutes.name: Amaretto

substitutes.notes: Nutty and sweet rather than cherry — different but works.

substitutes.name: Cherry syrup + almond extract

substitutes.name: Orgeat syrup

substitutes.name: Frangelico

substitutes.name: Bourbon + orgeat

substitutes.name: Elderflower cordial + vodka

substitutes.name: Lychee liqueur

substitutes.name: Elderflower syrup (non-alcoholic)

substitutes.name: Violet liqueur (Crème de Violette)

substitutes.name: Amaretto

substitutes.name: Almond milk + simple syrup

substitutes.name: Falernum

substitutes.name: Homemade orgeat

substitutes.name: Coconut milk + simple syrup

substitutes.name: Coconut cream (unsweetened) + sugar

substitutes.name: Orgeat
